title.en: The Gold Axe and Silver Axe

summary: A humble woodcutter loses his father’s old iron axe and chooses honesty when a mountain spirit offers shining gold and silver instead.
sensitivityNotes: No physical danger beyond a dropped tool.
Greed is corrected through a gentle lesson.

text.enLittle: Long ago, in a blue-green valley, a woodcutter polished an old iron axe every morning.
text.enStandard: Long ago, in a blue-green valley, a woodcutter polished an old iron axe every morning. It had belonged to his father, and it fit his hands like a memory.
text.koStandard: 옛날 푸른 골짜기에 나무꾼이 살았어요. 그는 아침마다 낡은 쇠도끼를 닦았지요. 그 도끼는 아버지가 쓰던 것이어서 손에 추억처럼 꼭 맞았어요.

pages.koreanText: 옛날 푸른 골짜기에 나무꾼이 살았어요. 그는 아침마다 낡은 쇠도끼를 닦았지요. 그 도끼는 아버지가 쓰던 것이어서 손에 추억처럼 꼭 맞았어요.
pages.englishText: Long ago, in a blue-green valley, a woodcutter polished an old iron axe every morning. It had belonged to his father, and it fit his hands like a memory.
pages.narrationScript: 옛날 푸른 골짜기에 나무꾼이 살았어요. 그는 아침마다 낡은 쇠도끼를 닦았지요. 그 도끼는 아버지가 쓰던 것이어서 손에 추억처럼 꼭 맞았어요.

vocabulary.definitionEn: A tool used to chop wood.
A plain strong axe made of iron.

storyBeat.pageTurnHook: The old axe matters before it is lost. The pond is about to ask whether an old tool can still matter most.
storyBeat.readAloudCue: Read with gentle and honest, with quiet temptation, warm relief, and respect for the old iron axe; keep pauses clear and musical. slow, respectful

text.enLittle: He used the axe only for fallen branches and trees already ready to give wood.
text.enStandard: He used the axe only for fallen branches and trees already ready to give wood. “Take only what you need,” he told the forest.
text.koLittle: 나무꾼은 쓰러진 가지와 때가 된 나무만 베었어요.
text.koStandard: 나무꾼은 쓰러진 가지와 때가 된 나무만 베었어요. “필요한 만큼만 가져갈게요.” 그는 숲에 조용히 말했지요.

pages.koreanText: 나무꾼은 쓰러진 가지와 때가 된 나무만 베었어요. “필요한 만큼만 가져갈게요.” 그는 숲에 조용히 말했지요.
pages.englishText: He used the axe only for fallen branches and trees already ready to give wood. “Take only what you need,” he told the forest.
pages.narrationScript: 나무꾼은 쓰러진 가지와 때가 된 나무만 베었어요. “필요한 만큼만 가져갈게요.” 그는 숲에 조용히 말했지요.

text.enLittle: His old axe slipped into the pond — plunk!
text.enStandard: One misty day, his axe slipped — plunk! Down it sank into the pond, taking his work, his memory, and his supper with it.
text.koStandard: 안개 낀 어느 날, 도끼가 손에서 미끄러졌어요. 풍덩! 도끼는 일거리와 추억과 저녁 밥걱정까지 데리고 연못 속으로 가라앉았지요.

pages.koreanText: 안개 낀 어느 날, 도끼가 손에서 미끄러졌어요. 풍덩! 도끼는 일거리와 추억과 저녁 밥걱정까지 데리고 연못 속으로 가라앉았지요.

text.enLittle: The woodcutter knelt by the water.
text.enStandard: The woodcutter knelt by the water. “Old friend,” he whispered, “how will I bring you home?”
text.koStandard: 나무꾼은 연못가에 무릎을 꿇었어요. “오랜 친구야.” 그가 속삭였어요. “어떻게 널 집에 데려가지?”

text.enLittle: The pond began to shine.
text.enStandard: The pond began to shine. Up rose a mountain spirit with sleeves like mist and eyes as kind as old stones.
text.koStandard: 연못이 은은히 빛나기 시작했어요. 안개 같은 소매와 오래된 돌처럼 다정한 눈을 가진 산신령이 물 위로 올라왔지요.

text.enLittle: “Did you drop this?” asked the spirit, lifting an axe of gold.
text.enStandard: “Did you drop this?” asked the spirit, lifting an axe of gold. It shone so brightly the woodcutter forgot to breathe for one small blink.
text.koLittle: “이 도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?” 산신령이 금도끼를 들어 보였어요.
text.koStandard: “이 도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?” 산신령이 금도끼를 들어 보였어요. 너무 반짝여서 나무꾼은 아주 잠깐 숨 쉬는 것도 잊었지요.

pages.koreanText: “이 도끼를 빠뜨렸느냐?” 산신령이 금도끼를 들어 보였어요. 너무 반짝여서 나무꾼은 아주 잠깐 숨 쉬는 것도 잊었지요.

text.enLittle: Then he looked at his hands.
text.enStandard: Then he looked at his hands. They remembered the weight of the old iron axe. “No, thank you. My axe is the old iron one.”

text.enLittle: The spirit smiled and lifted a silver axe.

text.enLittle: At last the spirit lifted a plain iron axe, nicked at the edge and dark from years of work.
text.enStandard: At last the spirit lifted a plain iron axe, nicked at the edge and dark from years of work. The woodcutter’s face lit like sunrise.

text.enLittle: The spirit laughed softly.
text.enStandard: The spirit laughed softly. “A truthful hand can carry more than treasure.” He gave the woodcutter all three axes.

text.enLittle: The woodcutter bowed low.
text.enStandard: The woodcutter bowed low. He sold the gold axe, repaired the village bridge, and kept the iron axe for ordinary mornings.
